description:
The deeper smell named while landing .10: three passes (reducer,
typer's walk_spec_for_discovery, ir_inline) each re-implement the
mapping from a block's terminator to "what callsites does it
produce, in which slots, with which targets/keys." The outcomes
table unified the *answers*; this ticket unifies the *question*.

## What to add

A single enumerator in a new module (likely `src/callsite_walk.rs`)
or as a method on `Block`:

```rust
pub struct Callsite<'a> {
    pub id: CallsiteId,           // (caller_fn, block, slot)
    pub target: FnId,
    pub args: &'a [Var],
    pub captures: &'a [Descr],    // empty for non-closure_lit slots
}

pub fn block_callsites<'a>(
    block: &'a Block,
    caller: FnId,
    env: &'a HashMap<Var, Descr>,
) -> impl Iterator<Item = Callsite<'a>>
```

One match site that knows:
- `Term::Call`        â†’ `Direct` + `Cont`
- `Term::TailCall`    â†’ `Direct`
- `Term::CallClosure` â†’ `ClosureLit(c,s)` per resolved sig OR
                        `CallClosureKnown` (via fn_constants) + `Cont`
- `Term::TailCallClosure` â†’ same as above without `Cont`
- `Prim::MakeClosure` (per stmt) â†’ `MakeClosure(stmt_idx)`,
                        gated on opaque-arity liveness (deferred to
                        caller â€” `block_callsites` may not have this)

The enumerator stays close to current `walk_spec_for_discovery`
structure â€” same Descr-key derivation logic, just hoisted out.

## What to refactor (mechanical)

- `ir_reducer::reduce_block` calls `block_callsites` to find calls,
  attempts fold per-item.
- `walk_spec_for_discovery`'s per-block terminator scan calls
  `block_callsites` to find emit targets. The env-building and
  Descr-keying logic stays in the typer (it consumes the
  enumerator's items + its own `block_envs`).
- `ir_inline` â€” out of scope here; absorbed under .E.

## Acceptance

- Every golden in `fixtures/` is byte-identical to current main.
- All 842 tests green; CI lint + clippy clean.
- New unit tests on `block_callsites` covering each terminator kind.
- `ir_reducer.rs` and `ir_typer.rs` have the per-Term::* match arms
  replaced by `for cs in block_callsites(...) { ... }`.

## What this unblocks

- `.10` becomes mechanical (typer just iterates).
- `.E` (reducer learns inline) is a single branch in the
  enumeration consumer.
- `.F.1` (boundary uniformity) is a one-line check inside
  `block_callsites` ("if `target âˆˆ boundary_fns` then yield Stalled
  hint").
- `Cont` slots become first-class outcomes (no longer awkward
  second-class).

## What dies

Direct walks-of-terminators in `reduce_block` / `reduce_terminator`
and `walk_spec_for_discovery`. The match-on-Term logic collapses
into one site.
title: fz-CO.D.0 â€” Extract block_callsites enumerator (shared by reducer/typer)
